summary: Phase 2 backend tests added and executed. 48/48 tests PASSED including all Phase 2 features (discount codes CRUD + validation, admin chat with GPT-5.2 + context, order status updates, product supplier/brand fields, checkout with discount code). Phase 1 regressions (auth, products, cart, orders, payments, /chat) all still passing — confirming main agent's fixes from iteration 1 (root route ordering, _id leak, payment status graceful 200) hold.

minor.issue: When discount_code is supplied but invalid (wrong code, inactive, expired usage limit, or min_order not met), endpoint silently ignores it and applies no discount with no warning to client. Could surprise users. Suggest returning a `discount_warning` field or 400 when code is non-empty but unusable so frontend can show a Latvian error toast.

discount_code used_count is incremented on checkout INITIATION (Stripe session creation), not on actual payment success. If user abandons checkout, the counter still increases. Consider moving the $inc to payment_status='paid' branch in /payments/status or webhook.
Still returns 200 instead of 201 (carried over from iteration 1, not breaking).
No brute-force lockout after 5 failed attempts (playbook recommendation, carried over from iteration 1).

action_items: (Optional) Surface invalid-discount feedback in /payments/checkout response so UI can show 'kods nav derīgs' rather than silently dropping it.
(Optional) Move discount used_count increment to payment-success path (webhook or /payments/status when payment_status==paid) so abandoned checkouts don't burn usage.
(Optional, carried) Brute-force lockout on /auth/login and 201 status on /auth/register.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py is now ~975 lines — well past the 700-line guideline. Should be split into routers (auth, products, cart, orders, discounts, payments, chat, admin) and a services layer.
Admin chat endpoint catches all Exception and returns 500 with the raw error message in detail — risks leaking provider/key info. Wrap with a generic Latvian message and log internally.
Order status enum is duplicated as a hardcoded list in update_order_status — should be a Pydantic Literal type/Enum so validation lives in the model.
PUT /api/orders/{id}/status accepts a free-form Dict[str,str] body; consider a Pydantic model OrderStatusUpdate for consistency with rest of API.

Checkout creates the order doc BEFORE talking to Stripe but increments discount.used_count AFTER session creation succeeds — fine, but combining business writes with external API calls in a single request is fragile. Wrap in a transactional pattern or compensate on failure.
DiscountCode.value typed as float but admin can pass int (e.g. 10) → Pydantic coerces — fine for now, but UI must format display consistently.
_notify_suppliers_for_order is currently log-only (MOCKED). Real email integration via Resend/SendGrid is pending — flagged for product owner.
test_credentials: admin@veikals.lv / Admin123! (admin); customers created dynamically as TEST_*@example.com.
seed_data_creation: Used existing seeded data: 6 products + 3 discount codes (VEIKALS10, IPHONE50, WELCOME). Tests create ephemeral TEST_ users, TEST_ products, TEST_ discount codes (and clean up via DELETE). Multiple Stripe sessions + pending orders created during checkout-with-discount tests (left for admin to observe in /orders).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend pytest at /app/backend/tests/backend_test.py covers 48 tests across health, auth, products, cart, orders, payments, discount codes (CRUD + validate), order status, supplier fields, checkout with discount, admin chat (GPT-5.2 with context), customer chat. Re-run: `pytest /app/backend/tests/backend_test.py -v --junitxml=/app/test_reports/pytest/pytest_results.xml`. All Phase 1 + Phase 2 backend functionality is verified working. NOTE: Supplier email notification is MOCKED (logged + persisted to db.supplier_notifications, no real email sent). Admin AI chat uses GPT-5.2 via emergentintegrations EMERGENT_LLM_KEY, takes ~5-15s per call.
rca of the issue: No bugs found in Phase 2 features. All endpoints validated end-to-end.
